sections.paragraph:
ἀνεπιδεής, ές (ἐπιδεής ‘in need of’; Pla., Leg. 12 p. 947e; Lucian, Dial. Mort. 26, 2) pert. to not lacking anything, lacking nothing of God (Philo, Plant. 35; Ath. 29, 2 τὸ θεῖον) PtK 2 p. 13, 25.—DELG s.v. 2 δέω.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
